name: EventBus Middleware
requirements: EventBus spec (to host the middleware stack)

userStory.iWant: to intercept, log, or modify messages before they reach my update function
userStory.soThat: I can build global analytics, time-travel debugging, and debouncing without polluting my core logic.

acceptanceCriteria: A middleware stack can be registered with the EventBus
Middleware functions can intercept and modify any BusMsg
Middleware functions can halt message propagation (short-circuiting)
Middleware functions can dispatch asynchronous side-effects (e.g. logging to a port)
backwardCompatibility: Legacy apps will simply have an empty middleware stack, with zero performance impact for standard message propagation.

inScope: Next() function pattern for sequential middleware execution
Global message logger implementation as an example
outScope: Individual application-specific middleware (left to the user)

testPlan.goldenPath: Register a middleware that converts all 'a' keypresses to 'b'. Emit an 'a' message and assert that the application's update function receives a 'b'.
edgeCases: Middleware that throws an error (should fail gracefully and log)
Middleware that hangs (should have a timeout or be identifiable in devtools)
Middleware that dispatches a new message resulting in a loop
testPlan.fuzzStress: Dispatch 10,000 messages through a stack of 50 middleware functions and measure the latency added to the TEA loop.

estimatedComplexity.timeComplexity: O(M) where M is number of registered middleware
